steps.title: Identify what an effective introduction requires
steps.content: The goal is to introduce the Slow Food movement to a new audience. That means the choice must convey the movement's core purpose and values — not just a date, not a single detail, and not an incomplete picture. The notes list four pillars: universal access to healthy high-quality food, sustainable production, fair treatment of workers, and fair compensation.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The international Slow Food movement, founded in 1989, promotes universal access to healthy, high-quality food that is produced sustainably by workers who are treated and compensated fairly.': Covers all four core pillars in one sentence and anchors the movement with its founding year — a complete introduction for a new audience. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The signing of the "Slow Food Manifesto" in 1989 marked the founding of the international Slow Food movement, while the Slow Food USA organization was founded in 2000.': Only gives founding dates; says nothing about what the movement stands for. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The Slow Food movement advocates for food production workers.': Captures only one of the four pillars; far too narrow to introduce the movement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Goals of the movement include universal access to healthy, high-quality food and sustainable food practices.': Omits fair treatment and compensation of workers, missing a key pillar. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
